questions.question: What is the term for the process by which a single gene influences two or more seemingly unrelated phenotypic traits?
A couple has a child with cystic fibrosis, an autosomal recessive disorder. If both parents are carriers, what is the probability that their next child will be a carrier?
What is the primary mechanism by which genetic variation is introduced into a population?

A population of birds has a beak size that is normally distributed with a mean of 10 cm and a standard deviation of 1 cm. If the population is under selection pressure to have larger beaks, what will be the result on the population's beak size distribution?

options.A: The mean will increase, but the standard deviation will remain the same
options.B: The mean will decrease, but the standard deviation will remain the same
options.C: The mean will increase, and the standard deviation will also increase
options.D: The mean will remain the same, but the standard deviation will decrease

questions.question: What is the term for the phenomenon where a dominant allele has a greater effect on the phenotype when it is paired with another dominant allele, rather than with a recessive allele?
A genetic disorder is caused by a frameshift mutation in a gene. What type of mutation is most likely to occur to restore the normal reading frame?

options.D: Another frameshift mutation of the same size but in the opposite direction

questions.question: What is the term for the movement of an individual with a unique genotype into a new population, resulting in the introduction of new alleles?

A researcher is studying the evolution of a population of bacteria. She observes that the population has developed resistance to a particular antibiotic. What is the most likely explanation for this observation?
What is the term for the phenomenon where two or more different alleles have an equal effect on the phenotype?

A population of rabbits has a gene for coat color that has two alleles: B (black) and b (white). The genotype BB or Bb results in a black coat, while the genotype bb results in a white coat. If a black rabbit with the genotype Bb is crossed with a white rabbit with the genotype bb, what is the probability that their offspring will be black?
What is the term for the process by which a population becomes reproductively isolated from other populations, resulting in the formation of a new species?
A genetic disorder is caused by a nonsense mutation in a gene. What type of mutation is most likely to occur to restore the normal function of the gene?

options.D: Another nonsense mutation in the same gene

questions.question: What is the term for the phenomenon where a single gene has multiple effects on the phenotype, resulting in a correlation between different traits?

A researcher is studying the genetic variation in a population of plants. She observes that the population has a high level of genetic diversity. What is the most likely explanation for this observation?
What is the term for the process by which a population becomes fixed for a particular allele, resulting in the loss of genetic variation?
A couple has a child with a genetic disorder caused by a dominant allele. If the father is normal and the mother is a carrier, what is the probability that their next child will have the disorder?
What is the term for the phenomenon where a population has a higher fitness than expected, due to the interaction between different genes?
A genetic disorder is caused by a deletion of a gene. What type of mutation is most likely to occur to restore the normal function of the gene?
What is the term for the process by which a population becomes adapted to a new environment, resulting in the evolution of new traits?

A researcher is studying the genetic variation in a population of animals. She observes that the population has a low level of genetic diversity. What is the most likely explanation for this observation?
